cases.description: Positive fractional number truncates its decimal part
Documented classic divergence from TRUNC: INT always rounds DOWN to the nearest integer (toward negative infinity), so a negative fractional number rounds AWAY from zero
cases.expected_note: Per Microsoft's INT function docs, INT(-8.9) = -9 because INT rounds down toward negative infinity, unlike TRUNC(-8.9) = -8 which truncates toward zero -- see the paired TRUNC_negative_truncates_toward_zero case in TRUNC.json for the direct comparison

cases.description: A whole number is returned unchanged
A whole negative number is returned unchanged (no fractional part to drop)
